result.narrative: The token name is a combination of the popular AI-created 'Daodun Dog' meme image from Douyin and the English internet dog meme 'what the dog doing?', and official tweets mention that NetEase Games added a Daodun Dog monster character and created a character script. The narrative promotes breakout through community spontaneous creation, KOL meme-playing, and cross-platform dissemination, using language confusion and dog-related memes to create dissemination points.

The ALONSHOUSE token is inspired by the address 46 Tongdean Avenue, associated with the mansion of Pump.fun founder Alon Cohen in Hove, UK. Launched by the boycott_pumpfun community as a satirical protest against platform fees, it sparked viral spread and doxxing controversies by calling for on-site visits, and is promoted by KOLs and the crypto community.

Burnie Senders token draws inspiration from the image of Soviet spies during the Cold War, with Solana co-founder Toly referencing related content and humorously asking 'Sleeper for 2028?' which triggered its spread. The token parodies Bernie Sanders' advocacy for free everything and equality, and spreads rapidly on social media through KOLs and Meme culture, attracting users to create content spontaneously.

Doorbell Chud token (Harry) is inspired by the viral post of Doorbell Chud, which shows a man with glasses standing in front of a doorbell. The community promotes the token through humorous interactions and memes, and the active participation of KOLs and the crypto community accelerates its viral spread.

The token name is derived from the baby hippo 'イロハ' at Ueno Zoo, and its official tweet bio mentions being born on December 25, 2025 and growing up in the zoo. It conducts community communication by showing Iroha's daily growth and related hashtags through official tweets.

result.author: meridian

token_criteria.notes: Any token. Ratio expresses directional bias.

entry.notes: 75% token = bullish (sell on pump out of range). 75% SOL = bearish/DCA-in (buy on dip). Set bins_below:bins_above proportional to ratio.

range.notes: bins_below:bins_above ratio matches token:SOL ratio. E.g., 75% token → ~52 bins below, ~17 bins above.

exit.notes: Close when OOR or TP hit. Re-deploy with updated ratio based on new momentum signals.

The UNC token was launched by @fiboancki on pump.fun, representing a warm boomer meme targeting people over 30, with exceptions including back pain, collecting Pokemon cards, parents, or occasional incontinence. The creator airdrops tokens to favorite 'uncs' to promote random unc memes and a positive atmosphere in crypto trenches, fostering user interaction through meme contests and creative activities, emphasizing community-driven participation.

The token name 'イロハ' is inspired by Japanese kanji, with a logo featuring a baby hippo, and the project's official website is linked to a page related to Tokyo Ueno Zoo. This token drives FOMO effects on the SOL chain through KOL dissemination and real-time transaction data sharing, and has a strategic fee system for buybacks, strategic promotion, and donations to relevant zoos.

The Official 67 Coin originated from the '67' meme formed by viral videos on TikTok and YouTube, and was launched on the Pump.fun platform. The token deposits 67% of creator fees into a specific wallet for Discord contributors to vote, spreads on the X platform through the #67to67Billion hashtag, holds community activities such as sticker competitions, and has KOLs participating in promoting its spread.

result.narrative: Lobstar originated from an AI agent created by former OpenAI engineer Pash using OpenClaw technology, which was given 50,000 SOL with the mission to increase its value to 1 million US dollars, during which it accidentally transferred 5% of the token supply to a user who claimed their uncle had tetanus. The token publicly displays wallet progress through the @LobstarWilde social account, injects 5% supply and all fees into the wallet for trading and repurchasing, and executes tasks, issues rewards, and interacts with the community through an anthropomorphic role, including paying 20,000 US dollars to users who completed the tattoo challenge.
